title: Grade 8 Math
description: Pre-algebra: real numbers, linear functions, slope, Pythagorean theorem, transformations, and statistics.

lessons.title: Approximate irrational numbers
lessons.objective: Approximate the value of an irrational number, including π and square roots, and locate that value on a number line.

lessons.title: Convert and compare rational numbers
lessons.objective: Order a set of real numbers arising from mathematical and real-world contexts.

questions.prompt: Which is greater: 7/4 or 1.7?
questions.explanation: 7/4 = 1.750. Comparing 1.750 and 1.7, the greater value is 7/4.

questions.prompt: Which is greater: 3/6 or 0.35?
questions.explanation: 3/6 = 0.500. Comparing 0.500 and 0.35, the greater value is 3/6.

lessons.title: Proportional vs. non-proportional
lessons.objective: Distinguish between proportional and non-proportional linear relationships.

questions.prompt: Classify the linear relationship: y = 6x − 6
questions.explanation: A relationship is non-proportional when y = mx + b with b ≠ 0. Here b = -6, so it is non-proportional.

questions.prompt: Is the relationship y = 6x + 6 proportional or non-proportional?
questions.explanation: A relationship is non-proportional when y = mx + b with b ≠ 0. Here b = 6, so it is non-proportional.

questions.prompt: Is the relationship y = 3x proportional or non-proportional?
questions.explanation: A relationship is proportional when y = mx (b = 0). Here b = 0, so it is proportional.

questions.explanation: A relationship is non-proportional when y = mx + b with b ≠ 0. Here b = 1, so it is non-proportional.

lessons.title: Multi-step equations
lessons.objective: Model and solve one-variable equations with variables on both sides.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  4x + 18 = 3x + 28
questions.explanation: Subtract 3x from both sides: 1x + 18 = 28. Then 1x = 10. Divide by 1: x = 10.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  9x − 18 = 5x − 2
questions.explanation: Subtract 5x from both sides: 4x − 18 = -2. Then 4x = 16. Divide by 4: x = 4.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  9x + 4 = 7x + 12
questions.explanation: Subtract 7x from both sides: 2x + 4 = 12. Then 2x = 8. Divide by 2: x = 4.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  7x − 13 = 1x − 73
questions.explanation: Subtract 1x from both sides: 6x − 13 = -73. Then 6x = -60. Divide by 6: x = -10.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  9x + 19 = 6x + 49
questions.explanation: Subtract 6x from both sides: 3x + 19 = 49. Then 3x = 30. Divide by 3: x = 10.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  2x + 11 = 7x + 6
questions.explanation: Subtract 7x from both sides: -5x + 11 = 6. Then -5x = -5. Divide by -5: x = 1.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  3x − 5 = 5x − 27
questions.explanation: Subtract 5x from both sides: -2x − 5 = -27. Then -2x = -22. Divide by -2: x = 11.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  9x + 4 = 4x − 16
questions.explanation: Subtract 4x from both sides: 5x + 4 = -16. Then 5x = -20. Divide by 5: x = -4.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  4x − 12 = 1x + 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 1x from both sides: 3x − 12 = 15. Then 3x = 27. Divide by 3: x = 9.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  2x + 3 = 5x + 0
questions.explanation: Subtract 5x from both sides: -3x + 3 = 0. Then -3x = -3. Divide by -3: x = 1.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  4x + 12 = 8x + 60
questions.explanation: Subtract 8x from both sides: -4x + 12 = 60. Then -4x = 48. Divide by -4: x = -12.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  6x + 3 = 8x − 1
questions.explanation: Subtract 8x from both sides: -2x + 3 = -1. Then -2x = -4. Divide by -2: x = 2.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  6x + 20 = 7x + 21
questions.explanation: Subtract 7x from both sides: -1x + 20 = 21. Then -1x = 1. Divide by -1: x = -1.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  8x − 11 = 2x − 23
questions.explanation: Subtract 2x from both sides: 6x − 11 = -23. Then 6x = -12. Divide by 6: x = -2.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  6x + 10 = 8x + 6
questions.explanation: Subtract 8x from both sides: -2x + 10 = 6. Then -2x = -4. Divide by -2: x = 2.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  4x − 4 = 7x − 37
questions.explanation: Subtract 7x from both sides: -3x − 4 = -37. Then -3x = -33. Divide by -3: x = 11.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  8x − 17 = 6x + 1
questions.explanation: Subtract 6x from both sides: 2x − 17 = 1. Then 2x = 18. Divide by 2: x = 9.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  9x − 11 = 2x + 45
questions.explanation: Subtract 2x from both sides: 7x − 11 = 45. Then 7x = 56. Divide by 7: x = 8.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  8x − 3 = 5x + 3
questions.explanation: Subtract 5x from both sides: 3x − 3 = 3. Then 3x = 6. Divide by 3: x = 2.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  8x − 6 = 1x + 50
questions.explanation: Subtract 1x from both sides: 7x − 6 = 50. Then 7x = 56. Divide by 7: x = 8.

questions.prompt: Solve for x:  2x − 1 = 3x − 3
questions.explanation: Subtract 3x from both sides: -1x − 1 = -3. Then -1x = -2. Divide by -1: x = 2.

questions.explanation: Subtract 1x from both sides: 5x − 5 = 10. Then 5x = 15. Divide by 5: x = 3.

questions.explanation: Subtract 7x from both sides: 2x − 3 = 9. Then 2x = 12. Divide by 2: x = 6.

questions.explanation: Subtract 3x from both sides: 3x + 3 = -9. Then 3x = -12. Divide by 3: x = -4.

questions.explanation: Subtract 1x from both sides: 6x − 17 = 7. Then 6x = 24. Divide by 6: x = 4.

questions.explanation: Subtract 5x from both sides: 3x + 17 = 38. Then 3x = 21. Divide by 3: x = 7.

lessons.title: Identify functions
lessons.objective: Identify functions using sets of ordered pairs, tables, mappings, and graphs.

questions.prompt: Does the set { (2, -3), (5, 1), (4, -5), (6, -3) } represent a function?
questions.explanation: Each x-value is paired with exactly one y-value, so this IS a function.

questions.prompt: Does the set { (2, -4), (6, 10), (1, -6), (2, 1) } represent a function?
questions.explanation: Two ordered pairs share the same x-value but different y-values, so this is NOT a function.

lessons.title: Pythagorean theorem: missing side
lessons.objective: Use the Pythagorean theorem and its converse to solve problems.

questions.prompt: Find the length of the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 7 and 24.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 10 and one leg 8. Find the other leg.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 13 and one leg 12. Find the other leg.
Find the length of the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 9 and 12.
Find the length of the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 3 and 4.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 5 and one leg 3. Find the other leg.
questions.explanation: a² + b² = c² → 3² + b² = 5² → b² = 25 − 9 = 16. So b = 4.

lessons.title: Scatter plots and trend lines
lessons.objective: Construct a scatter plot and describe the observed data to address questions of association.

questions.prompt: Describe the association shown by this scatter plot: As the number of hours studied increases, test scores tend to increase.
questions.explanation: When one variable increases as the other increases, the association is positive.

questions.prompt: Describe the association shown by this scatter plot: Shoe size and quiz scores show no clear pattern.
questions.explanation: The variables don't move together in any consistent way, so there is no clear association.

questions.prompt: Describe the association shown by this scatter plot: As the outdoor temperature increases, hot-chocolate sales tend to decrease.
questions.explanation: When one variable decreases as the other increases, the association is negative.
